concepts.title: Author and Theme
concepts.description: The story 'Deep Water' is written by William Douglas, and the theme revolves around the author's experience of overcoming his fear of water, highlighting the importance of perseverance and self-confidence.

concepts.title: Inciting Incident
concepts.description: The line 'I was thrown into the deep end of the Y.M.C.A. pool' marks the beginning of the author's journey to confront his fear, as mentioned in the NCERT textbook.

concepts.title: Character Sketch
concepts.description: The board often asks for a character sketch of the author, focusing on his transformation from a fearful boy to a confident young man, making it essential to understand his character development throughout the story.

concepts.title: Sequence of Events
concepts.description: To remember the sequence of events, use the mnemonic 'FANS': Fear (initial fear of water), Accident (being thrown into the pool), Nurture (support from family and friends), and Success (overcoming the fear).

concepts.title: Confusing the Author with the Protagonist
concepts.description: Students often confuse the author, William Douglas, with the protagonist, assuming they are different characters. However, the story is an autobiographical account, and the author is the protagonist, making it crucial to avoid this mistake.

concepts.title: Key Events and Dates
concepts.description: Remember the key events and dates in the story, such as the author's age when he learned to swim (11 years old) and the time it took him to overcome his fear (around 2-3 years), to provide accurate answers in the exam.

concepts.title: Symbolism of Water
concepts.description: Water symbolizes the author's fear and anxiety, while also representing a challenge and an opportunity for growth, making it a crucial element to understand in the story.

concepts.title: The Role of Family and Friends
concepts.description: The board often asks about the role of family and friends in the author's life, highlighting their support and encouragement in helping him overcome his fear, making it essential to analyze their contributions to the story.
